_note: All 17 GIICA-managed venues. Figures in AUD. Sourced from Queensland Government Budget papers, GIICA official announcements, and ministerial media statements at statements.qld.gov.au. Last reviewed: June 2026.

venues.name: Brisbane Stadium
venues.sport: Athletics, Para Athletics, Opening and Closing Ceremonies

budget_entries.source_label: QLD Government — original Gabba rebuild estimate Feb 2021
budget_entries.notes: This was the original estimate for a full rebuild of The Gabba. The Gabba rebuild was cancelled in March 2024. Retained here for historical comparison.

budget_entries.source_label: QLD Government — 2032 Delivery Plan announced Mar 2025
budget_entries.notes: Gabba rebuild cancelled. New 63,000-seat stadium at Victoria Park confirmed. Cost reported as $3.4B to $3.8B AUD across multiple sources. Figure here reflects the midpoint. GIICA Chairman confirmed 'approximately $3.8B' in separate briefings.

budget_entries.source_label: Queensland Budget 2025-26
budget_entries.notes: $3.8B provisioned for total venues programme. Brisbane Stadium is by far the largest single item within this envelope. Federal Government contributing $1.2B toward stadium via the $3.435B funding deal.

venues.name: National Aquatic Centre
venues.sport: Swimming, Para Swimming, Diving, Water Polo, Artistic Swimming

budget_entries.source_label: Swimming Australia concept submission
budget_entries.notes: Initial $650M figure presented by Swimming Australia as part of the Games venue concept stage. Subsequently reviewed by GIICA.

budget_entries.source_label: GIICA Chairman Stephen Conry — revised estimate
budget_entries.notes: Revised to $1.2B following GIICA planning review. Conry described prior figure as insufficiently realistic. Scope remained largely consistent; cost increase attributed to more rigorous project costing. Second most expensive venue after Brisbane Stadium.

budget_entries.source_label: Queensland Budget 2025-26 — $847M minor venues package (proportional estimate)
budget_entries.notes: Individual cost not separately disclosed. $120M is a proportional estimate based on relative scope within the $847M package (new grandstand, 5,000 permanent seats). Not an official government figure.

venues.name: Brisbane Aquatic Centre
venues.sport: Aquatics (support venue to National Aquatic Centre)

budget_entries.source_label: 2032 Delivery Plan Mar 2025
budget_entries.notes: Upgrade to existing venue originally built for the 1982 Commonwealth Games. Will support the National Aquatic Centre at Victoria Park. Individual budget figure not separately announced.

budget_entries.source_label: 2032 Games Infrastructure Funding Deal Confirmed Jul 2025
budget_entries.notes: Listed as a venue with previously allocated federal funding in the revised Minor Venues Program. Individual cost not separately disclosed.

budget_entries.source_label: 2032 Games Infrastructure Funding Deal Confirmed Jul 2025
budget_entries.notes: Listed as venue with previously allocated federal funding. Individual cost not separately disclosed.

budget_entries.source_label: 2032 Games Infrastructure Funding Deal Confirmed Jul 2025
budget_entries.notes: UCI certified venue. Minor works confirmed as required. Listed with previously allocated federal funding. Individual cost not disclosed.

budget_entries.source_label: 2032 Delivery Plan Mar 2025
budget_entries.notes: Precinct-wide upgrades covering the Sleeman Sports Complex. Contains Anna Meares Velodrome, Brisbane SX BMX Centre, Brisbane Aquatic Centre, and Para Sport Facility. Costs reported per individual venue where available.

budget_entries.source_label: 2032 Games Infrastructure Funding Deal Confirmed Jul 2025
budget_entries.notes: New dedicated para-sport facility added to revised Minor Venues Program. Individual cost not disclosed.

budget_entries.source_label: Queensland Budget 2025-26 — $847M minor venues package (proportional estimate)
budget_entries.notes: Individual cost not separately disclosed. $180M is a proportional estimate based on relative scope within the $847M package (new multi-sport indoor facility). Not an official government figure. Procurement launched July 2025.

venues.name: Moreton Bay Indoor Sports Centre
venues.location: The Moreton Bay Central Precinct, Petrie, Moreton Bay

budget_entries.source_label: Queensland Budget 2025-26 — $847M minor venues package (proportional estimate)
budget_entries.notes: Individual cost not separately disclosed. $200M is a proportional estimate based on relative scope within the $847M package (new multi-sport indoor facility, largest of the three new indoor centres). Not an official government figure. Design phase underway as of June 2026.

budget_entries.source_label: 2032 Games Infrastructure Funding Deal Confirmed Jul 2025
budget_entries.notes: Expanded Queensland Tennis Centre added to revised Minor Venues Program. Individual cost not disclosed.

budget_entries.source_label: 2032 Games Infrastructure Funding Deal Confirmed Jul 2025
budget_entries.notes: New purpose-built whitewater venue. Listed with previously allocated federal funding. Individual cost not disclosed.

budget_entries.source_label: 2032 Delivery Plan Mar 2025
budget_entries.notes: Upgrade to natural flatwater venue on the Fitzroy River. Community consultation underway as of June 2026. Individual cost not disclosed.

budget_entries.source_label: Queensland Budget 2025-26 — $847M minor venues package (proportional estimate)
budget_entries.notes: Individual cost not separately disclosed. $97M is a proportional estimate based on relative scope within the $847M package (new outdoor trail facility, smallest scope of the five). Not an official government figure.

budget_entries.source_label: Queensland Budget 2025-26 — $847M minor venues package (proportional estimate)
budget_entries.notes: Individual cost not separately disclosed. $250M is a proportional estimate based on relative scope within the $847M package (largest of the five: stadium expansion from 1,046 to 10,680 permanent seats). Not an official government figure. Design phase underway as of June 2026.

budget_entries.source_label: 2032 Games Infrastructure Funding Deal Confirmed Jul 2025
budget_entries.notes: Formerly referred to as Toowoomba Equestrian Centre in early planning documents. Added to revised Minor Venues Program July 2025. Individual cost not disclosed.
